title: Giant Apes Vanish in Ancient Climate Shift
story: Towering apes once roamed southern China, dwarfing even the largest gorillas we know today. On January 11, 2024, scientists revealed the fate of Gigantopithecus blacki, a colossal primate standing 10 feet tall. These gentle giants vanished from Earth long ago, between 215,000 and 295,000 years in the past. The culprit? A changing climate that altered their forest home, leaving these plant-eaters struggling to find enough food.
activity_question: How tall was Gigantopithecus blacki?
follow_up_question: What caused these giant apes to go extinct?

title: A Life-Saving Discovery for Diabetes
story: Imagine being unable to eat sugar without getting sick! In 1922, a breakthrough changed lives forever. Dr. Frederick Banting made history on January 11 when he injected insulin into 14-year-old Leonard Thompson. This Canadian teen became the first person with diabetes to receive this revolutionary treatment. Before insulin, diabetes was often deadly. Banting's discovery gave hope to millions worldwide.

title: Amelia Earhart's Daring Pacific Flight
story: Amelia Earhart soared into history in 1935, becoming the first person to fly solo from Hawaii to California. On January 11, she took off from Honolulu, facing nearly a day of endless ocean. Battling winds and loneliness, Earhart guided her plane across the vast Pacific. After a grueling journey, she touched down in Oakland, proving that determination and skill could conquer even the mightiest challenges.

Land a plane on an aircraft carrier

title: A Visionary's Birth Sparks Communication Revolution
story: Ezra Cornell, a man who would change the world, took his first breath in 1807. Born in Westchester Landing, New York on January 11, this future innovator grew up to become a pioneering businessman. Cornell founded Western Union Telegraph, revolutionizing long-distance communication. His passion for knowledge led him to establish Cornell University, opening doors for countless students to learn and grow.

title: Nora Heysen: Trailblazing Artist in Australia
story: Brushes in hand, Nora Heysen painted her way into history. Born in Hahndorf, South Australia on January 11, 1911, she became Australia's first female war artist. Heysen's talent and determination broke barriers in the art world, inspiring future generations of creative minds.
activity_question: What achievement made Nora Heysen a trailblazer in Australia?
activity_choices: She became Australia's first female war artist
She invented a new painting technique
She opened the largest art gallery in Australia
She painted the tallest mural in Sydney
activity_answer: She became Australia's first female war artist
follow_up_question: Can you think of other fields where women have been trailblazers?

title: Schoolboy Rowe: From Texas to MLB Stardom
story: Lynwood Rowe stepped onto the baseball diamond and became a legend. Born in Waco, Texas on January 11, 1912, he earned the nickname 'Schoolboy' for his youthful looks. Rowe's powerful arm carried him to the major leagues, where he shone as an All-Star pitcher in 1935, 1936, and 1947. He even helped the Detroit Tigers clinch the World Series in 1935!
activity_question: What nickname did Lynwood Rowe earn?
follow_up_question: Why do you think a baseball player might be called 'Schoolboy'?

title: A Marathon Champion's First Steps
story: In 1876, a future Olympic hero took his first breath. Thomas Hicks, born on January 11 in Birmingham, England, would grow up to become a legendary runner. His crowning achievement? Winning the gold medal in the marathon at the 1904 Olympic Games. Hicks' determination and endurance made him a true champion of his time.
